drugs.u: Metformin is the first-line medicine for type 2 diabetes. It lowers blood sugar by reducing glucose production in the liver and improving insulin sensitivity.
drugs.s: Type 2 diabetes, High blood sugar, Insulin resistance, Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), Prediabetes
drugs.d: Adults: 500mg twice daily with meals, increasing gradually to 2000mg daily. Maximum 2550mg per day. Always take with food.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, diarrhea, stomach upset, metallic taste. Rare but serious: lactic acidosis.
drugs.w: Stop before surgery or contrast dye procedures. Avoid alcohol. Monitor kidney function regularly.

drugs.u: Atorvastatin lowers bad cholesterol (LDL) and triglycerides while raising good cholesterol (HDL). It reduces the risk of heart attack and stroke.
drugs.s: High cholesterol, High triglycerides, Heart disease prevention, Stroke prevention
drugs.d: Adults: 10-80mg once daily. Starting dose usually 10-20mg. Adjusted based on cholesterol levels.
drugs.se: Common: muscle aches, joint pain, nausea, constipation. Serious: muscle breakdown, liver problems.
drugs.w: Avoid grapefruit juice. Report muscle pain immediately. Avoid in pregnancy.

drugs.u: Omeprazole reduces stomach acid production. Used to treat acid reflux, heartburn, stomach ulcers, and protect stomach from NSAID damage.
drugs.s: Acid reflux, Heartburn, GERD, Stomach ulcers, Duodenal ulcers, H. pylori infection
drugs.d: Adults: 20-40mg once daily before breakfast. For ulcers: 4-8 weeks. For H. pylori: combined with antibiotics 7-14 days.
drugs.se: Common: headache, nausea, diarrhea, stomach pain. Long-term: low magnesium, low B12.
drugs.w: Do not use long-term without doctor advice. May mask stomach cancer symptoms.

drugs.u: Amlodipine treats high blood pressure and chest pain (angina). It relaxes blood vessels so the heart does not work as hard.
drugs.s: High blood pressure, Angina, Coronary artery disease, Chest pain, Heart disease prevention
drugs.d: Adults: 5mg once daily. May increase to 10mg daily. Takes 7-14 days for full effect.
drugs.se: Common: ankle swelling, flushing, headache, dizziness, fatigue.
drugs.w: Do not stop suddenly. Rise slowly to avoid dizziness. Avoid grapefruit juice.

drugs.u: Lisinopril treats high blood pressure, heart failure, and protects kidneys in diabetic patients. It works by relaxing blood vessels.
drugs.s: High blood pressure, Heart failure, Heart attack recovery, Diabetic kidney disease
drugs.d: Adults: 5-10mg once daily. Maintenance: 20-40mg daily. Adjust based on blood pressure response.
drugs.se: Common: dry persistent cough, dizziness, headache, fatigue. Serious: angioedema, high potassium.
drugs.w: Stop immediately if face or throat swelling occurs. Avoid potassium supplements. Do not use in pregnancy.

drugs.u: Levothyroxine is a synthetic thyroid hormone for hypothyroidism. It replaces the hormone the thyroid cannot produce enough of.
drugs.s: Hypothyroidism, Underactive thyroid, Goiter, Hashimoto thyroiditis
drugs.d: Adults: 25-50 mcg once daily, increasing gradually. Maintenance: 100-200 mcg daily. Take on empty stomach 30-60 minutes before breakfast.
drugs.se: At correct dose: minimal. Too much: palpitations, tremor, weight loss, insomnia, sweating.
drugs.w: Take on empty stomach. Many foods interfere with absorption. Never miss dose. Regular thyroid blood tests needed.

drugs.u: Cetirizine relieves allergy symptoms. It blocks histamine receptors to reduce sneezing, runny nose, itchy eyes, and skin rashes.
drugs.s: Hay fever, Allergic rhinitis, Urticaria (hives), Itchy skin, Allergic conjunctivitis, Dust allergy
drugs.d: Adults and children over 12: 10mg once daily. Children 6-12: 5-10mg daily. Can be taken with or without food.
drugs.se: Common: drowsiness, dry mouth, headache, dizziness, fatigue.
drugs.w: Avoid alcohol. Use caution when driving. Reduce dose in kidney disease.

drugs.u: Salbutamol is a fast-acting bronchodilator for asthma attacks and breathing difficulties. It relaxes airway muscles to make breathing easier.
drugs.s: Asthma attack, Shortness of breath, Wheezing, COPD, Bronchospasm, Exercise-induced asthma
drugs.d: Inhaler: 100-200 mcg (1-2 puffs) when needed. Maximum 4 puffs in 24 hours. Nebulizer: 2.5mg 3-4 times daily.
drugs.se: Common: trembling hands, fast heartbeat, headache, muscle cramps, nervousness.
drugs.w: Do not use more than prescribed. If needing more than usual, see doctor immediately. Shake inhaler before use.

drugs.u: Metronidazole treats anaerobic bacteria and parasites. Used for dental infections, bacterial vaginosis, amoebic dysentery, giardia, and H. pylori therapy.
drugs.s: Dental infections, Bacterial vaginosis, Amoebic dysentery, Giardiasis, H. pylori infection, Trichomoniasis
drugs.d: Adults: 400-500mg twice or three times daily for 5-7 days. H. pylori: 400mg twice daily for 7 days.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, metallic taste, dry mouth, headache, dizziness.
drugs.w: Absolutely avoid alcohol during treatment and 48 hours after — causes severe reaction. Complete full course.

drugs.u: Doxycycline is a broad-spectrum tetracycline antibiotic for chest infections, skin infections, urinary infections, STIs, and malaria prevention.
drugs.s: Chest infections, Pneumonia, UTI, Skin infections, Acne, Chlamydia, Malaria prevention, Lyme disease
drugs.d: Adults: 100mg twice daily on day 1, then 100mg once daily. Malaria prevention: 100mg daily starting 2 days before travel.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, skin sensitivity to sunlight, esophageal irritation.
drugs.w: Do not take with dairy or antacids. Stay upright for 30 minutes after taking. Use sunscreen. Not in children under 8 or pregnancy.

drugs.u: Ciprofloxacin is a fluoroquinolone antibiotic for serious bacterial infections including urinary, respiratory, and gastrointestinal infections.
drugs.s: Urinary tract infections, Kidney infections, Chest infections, Typhoid fever, Traveler diarrhea, Bone infections
drugs.d: Adults: 250-750mg twice daily. UTI: 250-500mg twice daily for 3-7 days. Serious infections: 500-750mg for 7-14 days.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, diarrhea, headache, dizziness. Serious: tendon rupture, nerve damage, C. difficile.
drugs.w: Avoid antacids, dairy, iron within 2 hours. Avoid excessive sunlight. Stop if tendon pain occurs.

drugs.u: Pantoprazole reduces stomach acid and treats GERD, stomach ulcers, and conditions where stomach produces too much acid.
drugs.s: GERD, Acid reflux, Stomach ulcers, Esophagitis, H. pylori infection, NSAID-induced ulcers
drugs.d: Adults: 20-40mg once daily before breakfast. For severe conditions: 40mg twice daily.
drugs.se: Common: headache, diarrhea, nausea, stomach pain. Long-term: low magnesium, vitamin B12 deficiency.
drugs.w: Do not crush or chew tablet. Long-term use should be reviewed regularly.

drugs.u: Losartan is an ARB for high blood pressure and kidney protection in diabetic patients. Unlike ACE inhibitors it does not cause dry cough.
drugs.s: High blood pressure, Diabetic kidney protection, Heart failure, Stroke prevention
drugs.d: Adults: 25-50mg once daily. Maximum 100mg daily. Takes 3-6 weeks for full effect.
drugs.se: Common: dizziness, fatigue, back pain. Serious: high potassium, kidney problems, low blood pressure.
drugs.w: Do not use in pregnancy. Avoid potassium supplements. Monitor kidney function.

drugs.u: Aspirin at low dose prevents blood clots to prevent heart attacks and strokes. At higher doses it relieves pain and reduces inflammation and fever.
drugs.s: Heart attack prevention, Stroke prevention, Headache, Fever, Pain relief, Inflammation
drugs.d: Pain/fever: 300-900mg every 4-6 hours. Heart protection: 75-100mg once daily. Never give to children under 16 for fever.
drugs.se: Common: stomach irritation, nausea, heartburn. Serious: stomach bleeding, allergic reactions.
drugs.w: Do not give to children under 16 with viral illness. Take with food. Avoid if allergic to NSAIDs. Increases bleeding risk.

drugs.u: Sertraline is an SSRI antidepressant for depression, anxiety, OCD, PTSD, and panic disorder. Increases serotonin levels improving mood and reducing anxiety.
drugs.s: Depression, Anxiety disorder, OCD, PTSD, Panic disorder, Social anxiety
drugs.d: Adults: 50mg once daily. Can increase to 100-200mg daily after 4 weeks. Take at same time each day with food.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, headache, insomnia, diarrhea, dry mouth, sweating, sexual problems.
drugs.w: Do not stop suddenly — taper dose slowly. Avoid alcohol. Takes weeks to work — do not stop early.

drugs.u: Metoprolol slows heart rate and reduces blood pressure. Used for high blood pressure, angina, heart failure, and irregular heart rhythms.
drugs.s: High blood pressure, Angina, Heart failure, Atrial fibrillation, Migraine prevention, Anxiety
drugs.d: Hypertension: 50-100mg once or twice daily. Heart failure: start 12.5-25mg daily, increase slowly.
drugs.se: Common: fatigue, cold hands and feet, slow heart rate, dizziness, sleep disturbance.
drugs.w: Never stop suddenly. Tell doctor if asthma. Monitor pulse rate regularly.

drugs.u: Furosemide removes excess fluid from the body. Used for fluid retention in heart failure, liver disease, and kidney disease. Also lowers blood pressure.
drugs.s: Heart failure, Fluid retention (edema), High blood pressure, Liver cirrhosis, Kidney disease
drugs.d: Adults: 20-80mg daily, taken in morning. Take early in day to avoid night urination.
drugs.se: Common: frequent urination, low potassium, dizziness, dehydration, muscle cramps.
drugs.w: Take in morning. Monitor electrolytes regularly. May need potassium supplements. Avoid dehydration.

drugs.u: Prednisolone is a powerful anti-inflammatory steroid for asthma, allergic reactions, inflammatory bowel disease, arthritis, and autoimmune disorders.
drugs.s: Asthma, Severe allergies, Rheumatoid arthritis, Inflammatory bowel disease, Lupus, Severe skin conditions
drugs.d: Adults: 5-60mg daily depending on condition. Usually taken in morning with food. Long-term: lowest effective dose.
drugs.se: Short-term: increased appetite, mood changes, insomnia, raised blood sugar. Long-term: weight gain, osteoporosis, cataracts.
drugs.w: Never stop suddenly after long-term use. Carry steroid card. Avoid chickenpox or measles contact. Tell doctors before any surgery.

drugs.u: Diclofenac reduces pain and inflammation. Available as tablets, gel, and injections. Commonly used for arthritis, sports injuries, back pain, and period pain.
drugs.s: Arthritis, Back pain, Muscle pain, Sports injuries, Period pain, Dental pain, Post-surgical pain, Gout
drugs.d: Adults: 50mg two or three times daily with food. Gel: apply 3-4 times daily. Maximum oral dose: 150mg daily.
drugs.se: Common: stomach upset, nausea, headache, dizziness. Serious: stomach ulcers, cardiovascular events, liver damage.
drugs.w: Always take with food. Avoid in heart disease, kidney disease, pregnancy last trimester. Use lowest effective dose.

drugs.u: Fluoxetine is an SSRI for depression, OCD, bulimia nervosa, and panic disorder. Has a long half-life making it easier to stop than other antidepressants.
drugs.s: Depression, OCD, Bulimia nervosa, Panic disorder, Premenstrual dysphoric disorder
drugs.d: Adults: 20mg once daily in morning. Can increase to 40-60mg daily. Takes 4-6 weeks for full effect.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, headache, insomnia, anxiety initially, dry mouth, sweating.
drugs.w: Do not take with MAOIs. Monitor young patients for suicidal thoughts. Avoid alcohol.

drugs.u: Warfarin is a blood thinner preventing blood clots from forming. Used in atrial fibrillation, artificial heart valves, DVT, and pulmonary embolism.
drugs.s: Atrial fibrillation, Deep vein thrombosis (DVT), Pulmonary embolism, Mechanical heart valves, Stroke prevention
drugs.d: Dose varies — adjusted to keep INR between 2-3. Typical starting dose 5mg daily.
drugs.se: Main risk: bleeding anywhere in body. Signs: blood in urine, black stools, unusual bruising.
drugs.w: Regular INR blood tests essential. Many food and drug interactions. Report any unusual bleeding immediately.

drugs.u: Tramadol is a moderate opioid for moderate to severe pain when other painkillers are insufficient. Works on opioid receptors in the brain.
drugs.s: Moderate to severe pain, Post-surgical pain, Chronic pain, Back pain, Cancer pain, Neuropathic pain
drugs.d: Adults: 50-100mg every 4-6 hours as needed. Maximum 400mg per day. Start low and increase gradually.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, vomiting, constipation, dizziness, headache, drowsiness. Serious: seizures, dependence, serotonin syndrome.
drugs.w: Risk of addiction. Avoid alcohol. Do not drive when drowsy. Do not take with SSRIs. Prescription required.

drugs.u: Gabapentin treats neuropathic pain, diabetic nerve pain, postherpetic neuralgia, epilepsy, and restless legs syndrome. Calms overactive nerve signals.
drugs.s: Neuropathic pain, Diabetic nerve pain, Postherpetic neuralgia, Epilepsy, Restless legs syndrome, Fibromyalgia
drugs.d: Adults: start 300mg on day 1, increasing over 3 days. Maintenance: 900-3600mg daily in 3 divided doses.
drugs.se: Common: dizziness, drowsiness, fatigue, weight gain, swelling, coordination problems.
drugs.w: Do not stop suddenly — taper dose. Causes drowsiness — avoid driving initially. Avoid alcohol.

drugs.u: Esomeprazole reduces stomach acid. Considered slightly more effective than omeprazole. Used for GERD, ulcers, and stomach protection.
drugs.s: GERD, Acid reflux, Stomach ulcers, Esophagitis, H. pylori eradication, NSAID-induced ulcer prevention
drugs.d: Adults: 20-40mg once daily 30-60 minutes before breakfast. H. pylori: 20mg twice daily with antibiotics.
drugs.se: Common: headache, diarrhea, nausea, stomach pain. Long-term: low magnesium, vitamin B12 deficiency.
drugs.w: Do not use long-term without medical supervision. Can mask stomach cancer.

drugs.u: Folic acid is essential for making red blood cells and DNA. Crucial before and during pregnancy to prevent neural tube defects. Also treats folate deficiency anemia.
drugs.s: Folate deficiency anemia, Pregnancy (neural tube defect prevention), Megaloblastic anemia
drugs.d: Prevention in pregnancy: 400 mcg daily before conception and first 12 weeks. Folate deficiency: 5mg once daily.
drugs.se: Very safe. High doses may mask vitamin B12 deficiency.
drugs.w: All women planning pregnancy should take 400 mcg daily. Women at high risk need 5mg daily.

drugs.u: Vitamin D3 is essential for calcium absorption and bone health. Also plays important roles in immune function, muscle strength, and mood. Deficiency is extremely common.
drugs.s: Vitamin D deficiency, Osteoporosis, Rickets, Bone pain, Muscle weakness, Immune support
drugs.d: Deficiency treatment: 50,000 IU weekly for 8-12 weeks then 1500-2000 IU daily. Maintenance: 1000-2000 IU daily.
drugs.se: Safe at recommended doses. Overdose: nausea, weakness, kidney stones, high blood calcium.
drugs.w: Get blood level checked before high-dose supplementation. Take with fatty meal for better absorption.

drugs.u: Vitamin B12 is essential for nerve function, red blood cell production, and DNA synthesis. Commonly deficient in vegetarians, elderly, and those taking metformin or PPIs.
drugs.s: B12 deficiency anemia, Pernicious anemia, Nerve damage from deficiency, Fatigue, Vegetarian supplementation
drugs.d: Oral: 50-150 mcg daily for maintenance. Severe deficiency: 1000 mcg daily. Injection: 1000 mcg monthly for pernicious anemia.
drugs.se: Extremely safe — excess is excreted. Rare: allergic reaction to injection.
drugs.w: Pernicious anemia requires lifelong treatment. Vegans should supplement regularly. Metformin users should have B12 checked annually.

drugs.u: Loperamide slows intestinal movement and reduces fluid loss in diarrhea. Used for acute, chronic, and traveler diarrhea and IBS-related diarrhea.
drugs.s: Acute diarrhea, Traveler diarrhea, IBS-associated diarrhea, Chronic diarrhea
drugs.d: Adults: 4mg initially then 2mg after each loose stool. Maximum 16mg per day.
drugs.se: Common: constipation, stomach pain, bloating, nausea, dizziness.
drugs.w: Do not use for bloody diarrhea or fever. Do not exceed recommended dose. Stop if no improvement in 48 hours.

drugs.u: Ondansetron blocks serotonin receptors to prevent and treat nausea and vomiting. Particularly effective for chemotherapy-induced nausea and post-operative nausea.
drugs.s: Chemotherapy-induced nausea, Post-operative nausea, Radiotherapy nausea, Severe vomiting, Gastroenteritis
drugs.d: Adults: 4-8mg up to three times daily. Chemotherapy: 8mg 30 minutes before, then every 8 hours for 1-2 days.
drugs.se: Common: headache, constipation, flushing, dizziness.
drugs.w: Can cause constipation. May cause QT interval prolongation at high doses.

drugs.u: Loratadine is a non-drowsy antihistamine for allergy symptoms including hay fever, allergic rhinitis, and urticaria. Safe for daytime use.
drugs.s: Hay fever, Allergic rhinitis, Urticaria (hives), Itchy skin, Allergic conjunctivitis, Pet allergy
drugs.d: Adults and children over 12: 10mg once daily. Children 2-12 under 30kg: 5mg once daily.
drugs.se: Very well tolerated. Rare: headache, drowsiness, dry mouth, fatigue.
drugs.w: Generally very safe. May rarely cause drowsiness — check response before driving. Safe in pregnancy.

drugs.u: Acyclovir treats infections caused by herpes viruses. Used for cold sores, genital herpes, chickenpox, and shingles. Early treatment is most effective.
drugs.s: Cold sores, Genital herpes, Chickenpox, Shingles (herpes zoster), Herpes encephalitis
drugs.d: Cold sores cream: apply 5 times daily for 5 days. Genital herpes: 200-400mg five times daily for 5-10 days.
drugs.se: Cream: local irritation. Tablets: nausea, headache, dizziness.
drugs.w: Drink plenty of water with tablets. Start treatment as early as possible. Wash hands to prevent spreading.

drugs.u: Fluconazole treats fungal infections including thrush, fungal nail infections, and serious systemic fungal infections. A single dose often treats vaginal thrush.
drugs.s: Vaginal thrush, Oral thrush, Esophageal candidiasis, Fungal nail infections, Cryptococcal meningitis
drugs.d: Vaginal thrush: single 150mg dose. Oral thrush: 50mg daily for 7-14 days. Nail infections: 150mg once weekly for 3-6 months.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, headache, stomach pain, diarrhea. Serious: liver damage (rare).
drugs.w: Tell doctor about all medicines — many interactions. Avoid in pregnancy especially first trimester.

drugs.u: Naproxen is an NSAID with longer duration allowing twice-daily dosing. Used for arthritis, menstrual pain, and acute pain. Better cardiovascular profile than some NSAIDs.
drugs.s: Arthritis, Menstrual cramps, Muscle pain, Back pain, Headache, Dental pain, Gout attack
drugs.d: Adults: 250-500mg twice daily with food. Maximum 1000-1500mg daily.
drugs.se: Common: stomach upset, heartburn, nausea, headache, dizziness. Serious: stomach ulcers, kidney problems.
drugs.w: Always take with food. Avoid in kidney disease, heart disease, pregnancy last trimester.

drugs.u: Allopurinol reduces uric acid production. Used to prevent gout attacks, kidney stones from uric acid, and high uric acid from chemotherapy.
drugs.s: Gout prevention, High uric acid (hyperuricemia), Uric acid kidney stones, Chemotherapy-related high uric acid
drugs.d: Adults: start 100mg once daily after food, increasing by 100mg every 4 weeks. Maintenance: 200-600mg daily. Drink plenty of water.
drugs.se: Common: skin rash — stop immediately. Nausea, diarrhea, drowsiness.
drugs.w: Stop immediately if skin rash develops. Do not start during acute gout attack. Drink 2-3 liters water daily.

drugs.u: Bisacodyl stimulates intestinal contractions for bowel movement. Used for short-term constipation and bowel preparation before procedures. Works in 6-12 hours.
drugs.s: Constipation, Bowel preparation before colonoscopy, Chronic constipation (short-term)
drugs.d: Adults: 5-10mg tablet at bedtime. Suppository: 10mg when needed. Do not crush or chew tablet.
drugs.se: Common: stomach cramps, diarrhea, nausea. Long-term: laxative dependence, electrolyte imbalance.
drugs.w: Short-term use only. Do not take within 1 hour of milk or antacids. Avoid in severe abdominal pain.

drugs.u: ORS replaces fluids and electrolytes lost in diarrhea and vomiting. Glucose aids sodium absorption making them more effective than plain water.
drugs.s: Dehydration from diarrhea, Dehydration from vomiting, Cholera, Gastroenteritis, Heat exhaustion
drugs.d: Adults: 200-400ml after each loose stool. Mix sachet with exact amount of clean water. Drink slowly if nauseated.
drugs.se: Extremely safe when mixed correctly.
drugs.w: Mix with exact amount of water. Use clean water. Do not add extra sugar or salt. Seek medical help if not improving.

drugs.u: Adrenaline is the life-saving emergency treatment for anaphylaxis. It reverses throat swelling, low blood pressure, and severe bronchospasm. Auto-injectors allow self-administration.
drugs.s: Anaphylaxis, Severe allergic reaction, Bee sting allergy, Food allergy anaphylaxis, Severe asthma attack
drugs.d: Anaphylaxis: 0.5mg IM into outer thigh immediately. Children: 0.01mg per kg. Repeat after 5-15 minutes if no improvement. EpiPen delivers 0.3mg automatically.
drugs.se: Palpitations, trembling, anxiety, headache, high blood pressure. These indicate medicine is working.
drugs.w: Inject into outer thigh through clothing. Call emergency services even after using. Carry two auto-injectors always.

drugs.u: Mebendazole kills intestinal worms including threadworms, roundworms, whipworms, and hookworms. Threadworm treatment requires treating all household members.
drugs.s: Threadworm (pinworm), Roundworm, Hookworm, Whipworm, Mixed worm infestations
drugs.d: Threadworm: 100mg single dose. Repeat after 2 weeks. Roundworm/hookworm: 100mg twice daily for 3 days.
drugs.se: Generally well tolerated. Common: stomach pain and diarrhea as worms die.
drugs.w: Treat all household members simultaneously for threadworms. Practice strict hygiene. Wash bed linen and underwear after treatment.

drugs.u: Clarithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic for chest infections, H. pylori eradication, MAC infection in HIV, and skin infections.
drugs.s: Chest infections, Pneumonia, H. pylori infection (peptic ulcer), Sinusitis, Skin infections, Otitis media
drugs.d: Adults: 250-500mg twice daily for 7-14 days. H. pylori: 500mg twice daily with PPI and amoxicillin.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, diarrhea, stomach pain, metallic taste, headache. Serious: liver problems, QT prolongation.
drugs.w: Many drug interactions — especially statins, digoxin, warfarin. Resistance to H. pylori increasing.

drugs.u: Nitrofurantoin is specifically used for urinary tract infections. It concentrates in the urine and is preferred for uncomplicated UTIs due to lower resistance than other antibiotics.
drugs.s: Urinary tract infection (UTI), Cystitis, Bladder infection, Recurrent UTI prevention
drugs.d: Treatment: 100mg modified-release twice daily for 5-7 days. Prevention: 50-100mg once at night. Take with food.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, vomiting, headache, dark yellow urine (harmless).
drugs.w: Only for UTI — not kidney infections. Avoid in kidney disease and at term of pregnancy.

drugs.u: Hydroxychloroquine treats malaria, rheumatoid arthritis, and lupus. Reduces inflammation and prevents disease flares in autoimmune conditions.
drugs.s: Rheumatoid arthritis, Lupus (SLE), Malaria prevention and treatment, Sjogren syndrome
drugs.d: Rheumatoid arthritis/lupus: 200-400mg once daily. Maximum 5mg per kg body weight daily.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, stomach upset, skin rash, headache. Serious: eye damage (retinopathy) with long-term use.
drugs.w: Annual eye exams essential with long-term use. Report any visual changes immediately.

drugs.u: Methotrexate at low weekly doses treats rheumatoid arthritis, psoriasis, and psoriatic arthritis. At high doses treats certain cancers.
drugs.s: Rheumatoid arthritis, Psoriasis, Psoriatic arthritis, Ectopic pregnancy, Crohn disease
drugs.d: Rheumatoid arthritis: 7.5-25mg once weekly (NOT daily). Always take folic acid 5mg once weekly.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, mouth ulcers, fatigue, hair thinning. Serious: liver fibrosis, bone marrow suppression.
drugs.w: MUST take folic acid. Weekly dose only — not daily (dosing errors cause deaths). Regular blood tests. Avoid in pregnancy.

drugs.u: Clotrimazole is a topical antifungal for athlete foot, ringworm, jock itch, and vaginal thrush. Disrupts fungal cell membrane causing fungus to die.
drugs.s: Athlete foot (tinea pedis), Ringworm, Jock itch, Vaginal thrush, Oral thrush, Skin fungal infections
drugs.d: Skin cream: apply 2-3 times daily for 2-4 weeks. Vaginal cream: 1 applicator at bedtime for 6 days.
drugs.se: Local: mild burning, stinging, redness, itching at application site.
drugs.w: Do not use in eyes. Complete full course even if improved. Keep area dry and clean.

drugs.u: Benzoyl peroxide kills acne-causing bacteria and reduces sebum. Available 2.5-10% strengths. One of most effective topical acne treatments without causing bacterial resistance.
drugs.s: Acne vulgaris, Mild to moderate acne, Blackheads and whiteheads, Infected acne spots
drugs.d: Apply once or twice daily after washing face. Start with 2.5% to minimize irritation.
drugs.se: Common: dryness, peeling, redness, stinging initially. Bleaches fabrics and hair.
drugs.w: Bleaches clothes and hair. Start with lower strength. Avoid eyes, lips, nostrils. Use moisturizer.

drugs.u: Permethrin treats scabies and head lice. Kills parasites by paralyzing their nervous system. Very effective when used correctly.
drugs.s: Scabies, Head lice (pediculosis), Body lice, Crab lice
drugs.d: Scabies: apply 5% cream to entire body from neck down, leave 8-12 hours then wash off. Repeat after 1-2 weeks. Head lice: apply to hair for 10 minutes.
drugs.se: Local: burning, stinging, tingling, redness. Safe for pregnant women and children over 2 months.
drugs.w: Apply to entire body including between fingers and toes. Treat all household members. Wash all clothes and bedding at 60 degrees.

drugs.u: Dexamethasone is a powerful corticosteroid for severe inflammation, brain swelling, cancer complications, and severe COVID-19. 25 times more potent than hydrocortisone.
drugs.s: Brain edema, Severe allergic reactions, Anaphylaxis, Severe COVID-19, Cancer-related inflammation, Meningitis, Croup
drugs.d: Anti-inflammatory: 0.5-10mg daily. Brain edema: 10mg IV then 4mg every 6 hours. COVID-19: 6mg once daily for 10 days.
drugs.se: Short-term: increased blood sugar, mood changes, insomnia. Long-term: adrenal suppression, osteoporosis.
drugs.w: Never stop long-term treatment suddenly. Monitor blood sugar. Many drug interactions.

drugs.u: Domperidone reduces nausea and vomiting and speeds up stomach emptying. Does not cross blood-brain barrier well so has fewer neurological side effects.
drugs.s: Nausea, Vomiting, Gastroparesis, Diabetic gastric stasis, Bloating and fullness, Reflux
drugs.d: Adults: 10mg up to three times daily before meals and at bedtime. Maximum 30mg per day.
drugs.se: Common: dry mouth, headache, diarrhea, hot flushes. Serious: cardiac arrhythmia at high doses.
drugs.w: Use lowest dose for shortest time. Avoid in people with heart conditions. Maximum 10mg three times daily.

drugs.u: Rifampicin is a key antibiotic in tuberculosis treatment. Also used for leprosy, meningococcal prophylaxis, and other infections. Always used in combination with other TB drugs.
drugs.s: Tuberculosis (TB), Leprosy, Meningococcal prophylaxis, Brucellosis, MRSA (combination)
drugs.d: TB: 600mg daily combined with other drugs for 6 months. Prophylaxis: 600mg twice daily for 2 days. Take on empty stomach.
drugs.se: Common: orange-red discoloration of urine, sweat, tears (harmless). Nausea, liver enzyme elevation.
drugs.w: Powerful enzyme inducer — reduces effect of many medicines including contraceptives. Monitor liver function. Orange body fluids expected.

drugs.u: Isoniazid is a first-line antibiotic for tuberculosis. Given for full 6 months of TB treatment. Also used for 6-9 months as preventive therapy in TB-exposed patients.
drugs.s: Active tuberculosis (TB), Latent TB infection prevention, TB exposure prophylaxis
drugs.d: Adults: 300mg once daily. ALWAYS given with vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) 25-50mg daily. Take on empty stomach.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, peripheral neuropathy (prevented by B6), liver enzyme elevation.
drugs.w: ALWAYS take with pyridoxine (B6) to prevent nerve damage. Avoid alcohol. Monitor liver function. Never miss doses.
